data.title: AACG: Revenue flat for FY 2025, but net loss widened amid competition and goodwill impairment
data.description:

data.title: Village Super Market hit with Wakefern counterclaims seeking exit from cooperative
data.description:
Village Super Market said Wakefern filed counterclaims seeking a declaration to compel it to sell its Wakefern stock and exit the cooperative.

Key Highlights:

  • Wakefern filed counterclaims and a third-party complaint on March 19, 2026, seeking a declaration of good cause to force Village Super Market to sell its stock and exit the cooperative.
  • Village Super Market says Wakefern has not alleged a board vote to compel the sale and believes there is no basis in the By-Laws for such action.
  • The company calls Wakefern's claims frivolous, intends to seek dismissal, and says the dispute has not materially impacted operations or financial performance to date.
  • Village Super Market cannot currently estimate the probability of outcome or any range of potential loss related to the litigation.

Original SEC Filing: VILLAGE SUPER MARKET INC [ VLGEA ] - 8-K - Mar. 25, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Tevogen Bio Holdings regains Nasdaq bid-price compliance after 1-for-50 reverse split
data.description:
Tevogen Bio Holdings regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price rule following a 1-for-50 reverse stock split.

Key Highlights:

  • Nasdaq notified Tevogen Bio Holdings on March 20, 2026 that it has regained compliance with Rule 5450(a)(1).
  • The compliance restoration followed a 1-for-50 reverse split of the company's common stock effective March 6, 2026.
  • Nasdaq closed the matter following the Notification Letter, allowing continued listing on the Nasdaq Global Market.

data.title: Nuburu Plans 70% Stake in Tekne Via EUR 16.7M Conversion, EUR 13M Injection and EUR 6M Buy
data.description:
Nuburu entered a letter of intent with the shareholders of Tekne to acquire a 70% equity stake, subject to Italy's Golden Power approval. The plan includes converting a EUR 16.692 million receivable into approximately 32.1% of Tekne, a EUR 13 million capital increase for 25%, and a EUR 6 million cash purchase for an additional 10%. The parties also outlined potential restructuring steps, including a facility purchase or lease in Ortona and a spin-off of certain non-core assets. Definitive agreements are to be negotiated in good faith.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Letter of intent for majority acquisition and related financing
  • Counterparty: Tekne and its shareholders
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 19 2026 / Mar 19 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Acquire majority stake and expand defense and drone manufacturing

Original SEC Filing: Nuburu, Inc. [ BURU ] - 8-K - Mar. 25, 2026

data.title: Noodles & Co. Shares Gain on Narrowed 4Q Loss, Comp Sales Growth
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Noodles & Co. surged after the company narrowed its fourth-quarter loss and posted a rise in comparable restaurant sales.

Shares were up 20% to $7.29 in after-hours trading on Wednesday, after closing down 7.5% at $6.06. At close, the stock was down 34% over the past year.

The fast-casual restaurant chain reported a loss of $6.8 million, or $1.16 a share, compared with a loss of $9.7 million, or $1.70 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, the company posted an adjusted loss of 43 cents a share.

Revenue ticked up to $122.8 million from $121.8 million a year prior.

Comparable restaurant sales rose 6.6% during the quarter. Company-owned restaurants saw a 7.3% increase in comparable sales, while franchises saw a 3.8% increase.

For the current first quarter, the company said it expects comparable restaurant sales growth of 9%. It is projected adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization more than doubling to between $5.7 million and $6.3 million.

The company expects full-year revenue of between $478 million and $493 million, with comparable restaurant sales expected to rise between 6% and 9%.

It expects between 30 and 35 company-owned restaurants and five franchised restaurants to close, while one or two franchise restaurants are expected to open. Capital expenditures are projected between $9.5 million and $10.5 million.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: BioLargo Secures $10 Million Equity Purchase Facility With Clearthink
data.description:
BioLargo entered into a committed equity purchase facility of up to $10.0 million with Clearthink, providing discretionary access to equity capital over a 36-month period once a resale registration is effective. Purchase notices are capped by the lesser of $500,000 or 300% of recent trading value per notice, with pricing set to the average of the two lowest daily closes over the prior eight trading days and a 9.99% ownership cap. As consideration, BioLargo issued 500,000 commitment shares to Clearthink. In a related move, BioLargo signed a registration rights agreement to register the investor’s resale of shares, a condition to commencing the facility.

Agreement 1: BioLargo Secures $10 Million Equity Purchase Facility With Clearthink

  • Agreement type: Committed equity purchase agreement for up to $10.0 million of common stock
  • Counterparty: Clearthink Capital Partners
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 20 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Up to 36 months
  • Reason: Provide flexible access to equity capital for working capital and general purposes

Agreement 2: BioLargo Enters Registration Rights Agreement to Support Clearthink Resale

  • Agreement type: Registration rights agreement for resale of common stock
  • Counterparty: Clearthink Capital Partners
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 20 2026 / same
  • Reason: Enable resale by investor to activate equity line

Original SEC Filing: BIOLARGO, INC. [ BLGO ] - 8-K - Mar. 25, 2026

data.title: Ballston Spa Bancorp Raises $26 Million via 7.375% Subordinated Notes Due 2036
data.description:
Ballston Spa Bancorp entered into Subordinated Note Purchase Agreements to issue $26.0 million of 7.375% fixed-to-floating rate subordinated notes due 2036 in a private placement. Interest is fixed at 7.375% to Apr 1, 2031, then resets quarterly to three-month SOFR plus 378 bps; interest is payable quarterly. The notes are redeemable at the company's option on or after Apr 1, 2031, subject to regulatory approvals, and are intended to qualify as Tier 2 capital. Proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Private placement of 7.375% fixed-to-floating subordinated notes due 2036
  • Counterparty: Qualified institutional buyers and institutional accredited investors
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 25 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until Apr 01 2036
  • Reason: Support general corporate purposes and regulatory capital

data.title: Boliden expects negative impact from seismic activity at mine on Q1 earnings
data.description:
Swedish miner Boliden said on Wednesday its earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization for the first quarter of 2026 will be negatively affected by about 400 million Swedish crowns ($42.77 million) after its Garpenberg mine experienced abnormally high seismic activity, impacting production.

($1 = 9.3518 Swedish crowns)

data.title: Boliden Says Production In Most Affected Part Of Mine Is Not Expected To Resume In 2026
data.description:
Boliden AB :

  • BOLIDEN - PRODUCTION IN MOST AFFECTED PART OF MINE IS NOT EXPECTED TO RESUME IN 2026

  • BOLIDEN - UNTIL FURTHER NOTICE, GARPENBERG'S PRODUCTION IS THEREFORE ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 30 PERCENT OF GUIDED CAPACITY OF 3.7 MTON PER YEAR

  • BOLIDEN - FOR Q1 OF 2026, EBITDA WILL BE NEGATIVELY AFFECTED BY APPROXIMATELY SEK -400 MILLION

  • BOLIDEN - Q1 THROUGHPUT WAS JUST UNDER 0.8 MTON INSTEAD OF EXPECTED SLIGHTLY OVER 0.9 MTON

  • BOLIDEN: HIGH SEISMIC ACTIVITY IN GARPENBERG DECREASED, MINING PRODUCTION AT LOW LEVELS WILL COMMENCE IN Q2

  • BOLIDEN - AVERAGE ZINC GRADE EXPECTED TO DETERIORATE SLIGHTLY; SILVER GRADE EXPECTED TO IMPROVE

data.title: China's X Financial Q4 revenue falls, net income drops
data.description:
Overview

  • China fintech platform's Q4 2025 revenue fell 14.1% yr/yr

  • Q4 net income dropped 85.2% yr/yr due to higher credit provisions and lower facilitation revenue

  • As of March 15, company repurchased $53.85 mln in shares and declared a semi-annual dividend

Outlook

  • X Financial expects Q1 2026 loan origination of RMB 14.5 bln to RMB 15.5 bln

Result Drivers

  • LOWER ORIGINATION VOLUMES - Co said Q4 loan origination and active borrowers declined due to focus on higher-quality origination and tighter credit standards

data.title: Spark Energy Minerals Advances Arapaima Project with Resumption of RC Drilling and Metallurgical Work
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - March 25, 2026) - Spark Energy Minerals Inc. (OTC Pink: SPARF) (FSE: 8PC) ("Spark" or the "Company") is pleased to provide an operational update on exploration activities at its flagship Arapaima Project, located in Padre Paraíso, within Brazil's Lithium Valley.

RC DRILLING PROGRAM - COMMENCEMENT AND SCOPE

The Company has mobilized to resume its next Reverse Circulation ("RC") drilling program at the Arapaima Project. The program is designed to include a minimum of 20 drill holes totaling approximately 2,000 metres, with each hole planned to an average depth of 100 metres. Drill holes are expected to be spaced approximately 300 to 400 metres apart and will target three priority exploration areas across the property.

Figure 1: Preliminary locations of planned RC boreholes for the approximately 2,000-metre drilling program. A total of 20 boreholes are planned, with an average depth of 100 metres, to support geochemical sampling and metallurgical test work focused on rare earth and gallium mineralization.

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

The primary objectives of the drilling program are to define and expand the Company's understanding of ionic adsorption clay ("IAC") mineralization hosting Rare Earth Elements ("REE") and Gallium, and to unlock additional exploration targets to support the continued advancement of the Arapaima Project toward a potential maiden inferred mineral resource.

This drilling program marks the beginning of an intensive exploration phase for Spark at Arapaima. The Company intends to provide regular updates as laboratory results are received from SGS Geosol. Initial assay results are expected following completion of drilling and laboratory processing, with subsequent results released as they become available, subject to laboratory turnaround times.

Analytical results will enable the Company to evaluate anomalous concentrations of REE and gallium and to conduct a more detailed interpretation of the geochemical signature, mineralogy, and geological behavior of the mineralized system.

METALLURGICAL TESTING - ANSTO LABORATORY

In parallel with the RC drilling program, Spark is advancing the design of a metallurgical testing of selected samples from drill holes that yielded the most significant results during the Company's previous drilling campaign (see press release dated February 13, 2026).

Sample preparation will include logistical planning for shipment to the ANSTO Minerals laboratory in Australia. Samples will be dispatched in batches and prepared as representative aliquots over two-metre intervals from drill holes exhibiting the strongest geochemical responses.

The objectives of the metallurgical testing program are to confirm the ionic adsorption clay character of the mineralized horizons and to evaluate preliminary recovery characteristics for REE and gallium along selected drill intercepts.

MAIDEN INFERRED MINERAL RESOURCE - PATH FORWARD

Upon completion of the RC drilling program and receipt of metallurgical test results, the Company intends to evaluate the technical data necessary to support a future maiden inferred mineral resource estimate prepared in accordance with National Instrument 43-101.

Spark is advancing the Arapaima Project in a systematic and disciplined manner, with the objective of establishing a well-defined critical-minerals resource in one of Brazil's most prospective emerging rare-earth districts.

"Spark is advancing systematically with the objective of a maiden inferred mineral resource at Arapaima. With drilling mobilized across multiple targets and initial metallurgical test work advancing, we are concurrently laying the technical foundation to support a potential future economic assessment at the appropriate time. If successful, this next phase will represent a critical step in translating our exploration results into a defined critical-minerals inventory in Brazil's Lithium Valley," said Dr. Fernando Tallarico, Chief Executive Officer of Spark Energy Minerals.

BRAZILIAN GEOLOGICAL SURVEY (SGB) VISIT

On March 6 and 7, 2026, the Spark team hosted a group of geoscientists from the Brazilian Geological Survey (SGB), led by Dr. Lucy Takehara Chemale. During the visit, the SGB and Spark teams exchanged technical knowledge on ionic-adsorption clay-hosted rare-earth and gallium mineralization.

The SGB team has been conducting regional research on rare earth mineralization environments in Brazil, and the Arapaima Project was of interest as an example of emerging critical minerals exploration within Brazil's Lithium Valley. The visit provided an opportunity to review local geology and discuss ongoing scientific research relevant to the project.

Figure 2. The Spark Energy Minerals team, with geoscientists from the Geological Survey of Brazil (SGB), during a technical site visit at the Arapaima Project.

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

QUALIFIED PERSON

The scientific and technical information disclosed in this news release has been reviewed and approved by Jonathan Victor Hill, BSc (Hons), FAusIMM, Vice President Exploration and Director, and Dr. Fernando Tallarico, P.Geo., Chief Executive Officer and Chairman of the Board, each of whom is a Qualified Person as defined by National Instrument 43-101 - Standards of Disclosure for Mineral Projects. Mr. Hill is a Director of Spark and is not independent of the Company. Dr. Tallarico is the Chief Executive Officer of Spark and is likewise not independent of the Company.

ABOUT SPARK ENERGY MINERALS INC.

Spark Energy Minerals Inc. is a Canadian company advancing the exploration and development of critical minerals essential to the clean-energy transition. The Company's primary focus is Brazil, where it controls a significant land position within the country's emerging Lithium Valley - a region recognized for its lithium, gallium, and rare-earth potential.

Spark's flagship Arapaima Project spans approximately 91,900 hectares and hosts multiple targets prospective for lithium and gallium-REE mineralization. Through systematic exploration, Spark aims to help strengthen the secure and sustainable supply of minerals that support electrification, renewable energy, and modern technologies. The Company is committed to responsible exploration practices and to supporting Brazil's development of a transparent and sustainable critical-minerals supply chain.

Neither the Canadian Securities Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the Canadian Securities Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

FOR ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, PLEASE CONTACT:

Spark Energy Minerals Inc.

Attn: Dr. Fernando Tallarico, Chief Executive Officer

Email: connect@sparkminerals.co

Tel: +1-877-272-9226

Website: www.sparkminerals.co

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Disclaimer

This news release contains "forward-looking information" within the meaning of applicable Canadian securities legislation. "Forward-looking information" includes, but is not limited to, statements with respect to the activities, events or developments that the Company expects or anticipates will or may occur in the future. Generally, but not always, forward-looking information can be identified by the use of words such as "plans", "expects", "is expected", "budget", "scheduled", "estimates", "forecasts", "intends", "anticipates", or "believes" or the negative connotation thereof or variations of such words and phrases or state that certain actions, events or results "may", "could", "would", "might" or "will be taken", "occur" or "be achieved" or the negative connotation thereof. This forward-looking information relates to, among other things, the ongoing and anticipated exploration work at the Arapaima Project, including the anticipated drill hole scope, spacing and targets; the evaluation of additional targets within the Company's land package; any statements regarding the interpretation of exploration results, the potential significance, continuity, extent, and grade of mineralization encountered; the objective to define and expand the Company's understanding of IAC mineralization hosting REE and gallium and the potential advancement of the Arapaima Project toward a maiden inferred mineral resource; the potential for future exploration and drilling programs; the implementation of the Company's future plans, including the proposed advancement of the Arapaima Project as currently contemplated; the intention to provide regular updates as laboratory results are received; the anticipated timing of assay results and the results and use thereof; the expectation that exploration activities, including drill results, will accurately predict mineralization; the anticipated sample preparation methods and shipment planning; the Company's intention to evaluate the technical data necessary to support a future maiden inferred mineral resource estimate; plans relating to a potential future economic assessment; the anticipated benefits of the Company's approach to exploration; and the Company's future plans, objectives, and strategies.

data.title: Lucid Diagnostics 2025 10-K: Revenue $4.7M, Net Loss $58.0M
data.description:
Lucid Diagnostics reported $4.7 million in revenue for the year ended December 31, 2025, with a reported net loss of $58.0 million and no per-share metric provided in the supplied text; the company recorded negative gross profit and substantial operating losses reflecting elevated commercialization spending. Revenue rose modestly versus 2024, driven principally by increased EsoGuard test consideration and volumes. The results reflect continued investment in sales, marketing, reimbursement and operational scale as Lucid advances adoption of its EsoGuard/EsoCheck solution.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $4.7 million (increase of $0.4M vs. 2024, principally from higher EsoGuard test consideration)
  • Gross Profit: $(2.0) million (reported)
  • Operating Income: $(43.6) million (reported operating loss)
  • Net Income: $(58.0) million (reported net loss for the year ended December 31, 2025)
  • Net Income Per Share: Not provided in the supplied text

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth: Modest revenue increase to $4.7M in 2025 from $4.3M in 2024, reflecting increased EsoGuard test volumes and consideration.
  • Commercial Expansion & Coverage Momentum: Activities include a Medicare LCD reconsideration submission, positive coverage decision from Highmark, an awarded VA contract, and recognition in NCCN guidelines to drive payer and provider adoption.
  • Channel & Deployment Milestones: Expanded provider programs (including Hoag), deployment of mobile test units, establishment of Lucid Test Centers, and approximately 12,000 real-world patient procedures demonstrating scalable in-office use.
  • Product & Clinical Validation: EsoGuard/EsoCheck supported by peer-reviewed studies, strong analytical performance data, 18-month real-world evidence and case reports highlighting early neoplasia detection.
  • Operational Scale & Staffing: Increased investment in sales, marketing and G&A to support commercialization efforts and expanded MSA services to scale clinical and reimbursement operations.

Original SEC Filing: Lucid Diagnostics Inc. [ LUCD ] - 10-K - Mar. 25, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: EWSB Bancorp 10-K: $11.106M Interest Income, $(5.93) EPS
data.description:
EWSB Bancorp reported total interest and dividend income of $11.106 million for the year, driven by higher loan yields and loan growth, while net income was a loss of $4.177 million, or $(5.93) per share, reflecting a full‑year tax charge that included a $3.298 million valuation allowance. Net interest and dividend income improved to $4.614 million as net interest margin expanded to 1.74%, but operating results were pressured by rising noninterest expense and a tax provision that turned the pre‑tax loss into a larger after‑tax loss. The results reflect a shift in the loan portfolio toward shorter‑term and adjustable‑rate products and changes in funding mix to support loan growth.

Financial Highlights

  • Total interest and dividend income: $11.106 million (up $1.368M year‑over‑year)
  • Net interest and dividend income: $4.614 million (up $1.241M; net interest margin 1.74%)
  • Provision for credit losses: $0.131 million (down $0.036M)
  • Total noninterest income: $1.758 million (up $0.060M)
  • Total noninterest expense (Operating Expense): $7.566 million (up $0.225M)
  • Income (loss) before provision for (benefit from) income taxes: $(1.324) million
  • Provision for (benefit from) income taxes: $2.853 million (includes $3.298M valuation allowance on deferred tax assets)
  • Net income (loss): $(4.177) million (loss widened $2.477M year‑over‑year)
  • Basic and diluted earnings per share: $(5.93), based on 703,811 average shares

Business Highlights

  • Revenue & margin momentum: Net interest income rose materially due to higher loan yields and an improved net interest margin, supporting core revenue growth.
  • Loan portfolio shift: Growth concentrated in home equity and construction loans as originations outpaced payoffs; management emphasized shorter‑term and adjustable‑rate loan focus.
  • Channel & funding mix: The bank shifted toward term deposits and increased usage of FHLB borrowings to offset deposit outflows and fund loan growth.
  • Operational efficiency & controls: ALCO policies were enhanced and interest‑rate risk modeling, deposit decay assumptions, and liquidity forecasting were upgraded.
  • Product & noninterest income: Mortgage banking income and life insurance cash value gains increased, partially offset by lower swap gains and reduced service fees.

Original SEC Filing: EWSB Bancorp, Inc. /MD/ [ EWSB ] - 10-K - Mar. 25, 2026

data.title: DT Cloud Star 2025 10-K: $0 Revenue, EPS $0.36 (redeemable), $(0.07) (non-redeemable)
data.description:
DT Cloud Star reported no operating revenue for the year and recorded a net income of $2,132,715 largely driven by interest and dividends held in its trust account; operating activities produced an operating loss of $(557,174). Rounded per‑share results were $0.36 for redeemable ordinary shares and $(0.07) for non‑redeemable ordinary shares, reflecting the special-purpose acquisition company structure and trust income. The company remains focused on completing an initial business combination to begin operating activities.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 (No operating revenues)
  • Gross Profit: $0
  • Operating Income (Loss): $(557,174)
  • Net Income: $2,132,715 (primarily from interest and dividends in Trust Account)
  • Net Income Per Share — Redeemable ordinary shares (basic and diluted): $0.36
  • Net Income Per Share — Non‑redeemable ordinary shares (basic and diluted): $(0.07)

Business Highlights

  • Business model: Cayman Islands blank‑check company formed to identify and complete an initial business combination.
  • Operational status: No operating revenues; primary activities include completing the IPO, searching for targets, conducting due diligence and preparing for the transaction.
  • Trust funds and timing: Majority of IPO proceeds held in a trust to finance a business combination; deadline extensions were obtained via sponsor deposits to maintain timing flexibility.
  • Major milestone: Entered a Business Combination Agreement with PrimeGen US, Inc. on February 2, 2026 to consummate the initial business combination.
  • Resource constraints: Limited cash outside the trust and reliance on sponsor support for working capital and transaction‑related expenses.

Original SEC Filing: DT Cloud Star Acquisition Corp [ DTSQ ] - 10-K - Mar. 25, 2026

data.title: Sienna Corporate Update
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - March 25, 2026) - Sienna Resources Inc. (FSE: A418KR), (the "Company" or "Sienna") wishes to announce that it has granted a total of 1.475 million stock options to its directors and consultants at an exercise price of twelve cents per share for a period of 12 months. The Company also granted a total of 3.05 million restricted share units (RSUs) to its directors, officers and consultants. The RSUs vest in one year from the date of grant. The options and the RSUs have been granted and vest in accordance with the Company's omnibus equity incentive plan and the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange therefore aligning management with the shareholders of the Company.

data.title: Cibus Stock Plunges on Public Offering of Shares, Warrants
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Cibus plunged after the company said it would launch a public offering of stock and warrants.

Shares fell 24% to $2.18 in after-hours trading, after closing up 5.9% at $2.87. At close, the stock was up 37% over the past year.

The agricultural technology company said it would use proceeds from the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes, including funding the development of its weed management traits in rice.

Cibus didn't say how many shares or warrants it would sell, or at what price.

It said that BTIG, the underwriter for the offering, will have a 30-day option to buy up to 15% of the shares included in the offering.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Meta Cuts Jobs While Expanding Executive Stock Incentives
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Meta on Wednesday, announced the layoff of approximately 700 employees as part of its ongoing strategy to reallocate resources towards artificial intelligence.

The majority of these layoffs are concentrated in Reality Labs, as well as in certain positions related to recruitment, sales, and Facebook operations.

These workforce reductions were implemented shortly after Meta unveiled a new stock compensation initiative for six senior executives, including Andrew Bosworth, Chris Cox, and Susan Li, which could potentially amount to $921 million each over a five-year period, contingent upon achieving aggressive growth targets.

Mark Zuckerberg has been intensifying Meta's focus on artificial intelligence, significantly increasing investment in infrastructure and talent while concurrently scaling back expenditures in less critical areas.

Meta has stated that the stock program aims to retain key leadership talent and align executives with the company's long-term growth objectives, which include a goal to elevate the company's valuation from approximately $1.5 trillion to $9 trillion by the year 2031.

The company has forecasted a minimum expenditure of $115 billion for the current year, predominantly allocated to AI infrastructure investments such as the establishment of new data centers, as it advances towards sophisticated AI systems and enhanced productivity through automation.

data.title: PLTH: Revenue declined and losses narrowed in Q4 2025, with strategic exits and new openings
data.description:
Q4 2025 saw revenue decline 16.7% year-over-year to $25.2 million, but net loss narrowed significantly. Full-year revenue fell 11.2% to $103.4 million, with a net loss of $63.9 million, including $29.8 million in impairments. The company exited California and opened new Florida dispensaries.
